## What happened
Wales Online published a veterinary warning on 6 February 2026 urging dog owners to act quickly if they suspect exposure to a “lesser known toxin” rather than waiting to see if symptoms worsen.

## Key facts
- Affected animals: dogs.
- The warning emphasised not delaying treatment for an unspecified toxin.
- Prompt veterinary care can improve outcomes.

## What pet owners should do
Seek immediate veterinary advice if your dog shows unusual symptoms after possible toxin exposure. Do not “wait and see,” and bring any suspected plant or substance sample to the vet.
